methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, though none provide sufficient overlap in stage or valuation tier for median peer analysis.

Given the lack of granular peer comparables at the Seed stage in this subsector, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $25M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $62M. This methodology produces a triangulated range of $53M (low) to $72M (high), centered on $62M.

The analysis is constrained by limited peer visibility at Persona's specific stage within robotics. While the sector contains established private players like 1X Technologies, Divergent, and Exotec, none provide direct comparability for valuation anchoring. The range reflects conventional step-up assumptions rather than market-derived multiples. Key uncertainties include burn rate relative to deployment progress, competitive positioning within the narrower frontier robotics market, and applicability of traditional stage multipliers to hardware-intensive frontier tech.
